items.headline: United Arab Emirates withdraws from OPEC effective May 1
items.body: The UAE announced it will leave OPEC stripping the cartel of its third largest producer. The country produces 3.4 million barrels daily and chose this timing because Hormuz closure limits market impact.

items.headline: Gaza holds first elections in twenty years with Fatah-backed candidates winning most seats
items.body: Municipal elections in Deir el-Balah gave Abbas-aligned candidates six of eight council seats. Turnout reached only 23 percent in Gaza compared to 56 percent in West Bank.

items.headline: Google signs classified Pentagon agreement authorizing AI use for any lawful government purpose
items.body: Google joined OpenAI and xAI in providing AI models for classified military operations. Over 600 Google employees signed letter opposing the Pentagon collaboration announced Monday.

es.headline: Princess Leonor will study Political Science at public university starting autumn 2026
es.body: Spain's heir to the throne will attend Carlos III University in Madrid after completing military training. The choice signals constitutional preparation over traditional royal curricula for future head of state.

no.headline: Norway's grid operator halts new industrial power connections above 5MW across northern regions
no.body: Statnett announced Wednesday all projects exceeding five megawatts north of Svartisen face indefinite postponement due to capacity limits. The freeze blocks major industrial development across Finnmark and Nordland for years or decades.

uk.headline: King Charles tells joint session of Congress that Ukraine support keeps citizens safer for generations
uk.body: The King urged continued military aid to Ukraine during a Tuesday address earning bipartisan standing ovation. His explicit defense of NATO and transatlantic security creates pressure on Trump administration amid Republican calls to end Ukrainian funding.

nl.headline: Dutch intelligence declares Netherlands faces highest threat level in eighty years from Russia and China
nl.body: AIVD published its annual report warning national security pressure from multiple adversaries exceeds anything since World War II. Russian hackers stole police data while China illegally acquired technology from Dutch companies threatening their autonomy.
